market_regime: Risk assets are consolidating after a strong 3-month run, with clear rotation out of high-multiple Technology (-8.8% 1m despite +26.6% 3m) and Communication Services into defensives and value: Health Care, Financials, Industrials and Utilities lead on a 1-month basis. The macro backdrop is a firmer dollar and rising 10Y yield (4.49%) under a hawkish Fed (Warsh/Hammack flagging inflation and possible hikes), while oil has collapsed ~29% in a month and gold has rolled over. Headlines warn of 'extreme speculation' even as the AI/data-center capex cycle remains the dominant structural driver.

themes.name: AI infrastructure & semiconductors
themes.thesis: Multiple headlines confirm an accelerating, multi-year AI capex cycle (Big Tech pouring 'trillions', data-center capacity forecast to surge from 2.3GW to 150GW by 2030, Nvidia citing $3-4T capex by 2030). This is a durable structural driver even though Technology just pulled back ~9% on the month, giving the trend layer a chance to re-enter on stabilization.

themes.name: Power, grid & electrification for data centers
themes.thesis: Congressional committees are advancing data-center and grid bills, and AI workloads are driving surging electricity demand. Utilities (+4.9% 1m) and power/grid equipment names are leading rotation as investors seek the 'picks-and-shovels' of the AI power buildout, a slower-moving and more defensive expression of the capex theme.

themes.name: Health Care defensive leadership
themes.thesis: Health Care is the strongest sector on a 1-month basis (+12.3%) and positive over 3 months, reflecting a rotation into defensives amid extreme speculation warnings and a hawkish Fed. Durable earnings and low cyclicality make this a natural momentum hunting ground if the tech-led melt-up stalls.

themes.name: Financials on rotation and higher-for-longer rates
themes.thesis: Financials lead alongside Health Care (+8.5% 1m, +6.4% 3m) as a rising 10Y yield and a firmer dollar under a Fed that may keep rates elevated support net interest margins and capital-markets activity. This is a rate-and-rotation beneficiary rather than a news-driven trade.

themes.name: Industrial automation & robotics
themes.thesis: Industrials are firmly in the leadership group (+5.8% 1m) and multiple headlines highlight accelerating humanoid/industrial robotics adoption (BMW deploying Figure AI, surging investor capital) alongside domestic semiconductor factory buildouts. Automation capex is a real, if earlier-stage, tailwind; robotics specifically remains hype-heavy, so confidence is moderate.

avoid.reason: WTI crude fell ~29% in a month and Energy is the weakest sector (-7.5% 1m); collapsing oil removes the earnings and momentum support for the sector.
Gold is down 6% on the month and ~18% over 3 months amid a rising dollar and rising real yields, an unfavorable backdrop for the trend.
Weakest major sector alongside tech (-3.2% 1m, -5.6% 3m); caught in the rotation out of high-multiple growth.

avoid.what: Speculative long-duration mega-cap tech at extremes
avoid.reason: Headlines flag 'extreme' speculation and Fed officials warn AI could fuel inflation and rate hikes; a hawkish Fed and rising 10Y compress rich multiples, so avoid chasing crowded names until the trend layer confirms re-acceleration.
